cases.title: Participant registers account with email and password via POST /auth/register
preconditions: API server running at /api/v1
Fresh unique email not yet registered in the system
No Authorization header required (public endpoint)
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with { email, password, displayName } for a new participant
Inspect HTTP status and response body
cases.expected: HTTP 200 or 201; response includes JWT token and profile with userId, email, displayName, and roles array containing exactly Participant; satisfies FR-32 participant self-registration with email and password

cases.title: Registration persists user row with normalized email and display name
preconditions: Auth module and users table available
No existing user with target email
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with mixed-case email, valid password, and trimmed displayName
Query users table for the normalized email
GET /api/v1/me with returned JWT
cases.expected: User row exists with email stored as lowercase normalized value; displayName persisted; GET /me returns matching profile; participant account created per domain model User entity

cases.title: Browser signup page creates Participant account and signs user in
preconditions: Web app running with dedicated /signup route
Fresh browser context with no stored JWT
Fresh unregistered email available
steps: Navigate to /signup
Enter email, password, and display name and submit the form
Observe post-submit navigation and TopBar account control
cases.expected: Account created with Participant role; user is signed in (JWT stored client-side per Flow 0); redirected to participant event discovery or intended route; TopBar account button shows display name

cases.title: POST /auth/register succeeds without prior authentication
preconditions: Fresh unique email
Request sent with no Authorization bearer header
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with valid { email, password, displayName } and no Authorization header
Verify account is created and JWT is returned
cases.expected: HTTP 200 or 201; public signup endpoint accepts unauthenticated requests per API design §2.6; account and session issued without pre-existing credentials

cases.title: Self-service signup assigns Participant role only and cannot elevate to organizer
preconditions: No existing account for test email
Database access to user_roles table
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with valid credentials and optional roles: ["OrganizerAdmin"] in request body if accepted by parser
Inspect returned profile roles and user_roles rows for the new userId
cases.expected: Profile roles contains Participant only; no OrganizerAdmin or OrganizerStaff role rows created; organizer accounts are provisioned via seed or admin invite, not public signup per roles-permissions §3.1

cases.title: Register-issued JWT sub maps to users.id for participant identity
preconditions: Fresh email for registration
Ability to decode JWT sub claim
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with valid credentials
Decode JWT sub from returned token
Compare sub to profile.userId and users.id in database
cases.expected: JWT sub equals users.id UUID; profile.userId matches; participantId in future registrations will map to this user id per roles-permissions JWT sub claim contract

cases.title: Signup persists password with adaptive hashing, not plaintext
preconditions: Database access to users.password_hash column
Fresh signup email
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with known password value
Read stored password_hash from users table for the new account
cases.expected: Stored value is bcrypt or argon2 adaptive hash (recognizable prefix and length); plaintext password is not persisted; satisfies NFR-17 password storage requirement

cases.title: Register response never exposes password hash or credential secrets
preconditions: Fresh email for registration
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with valid credentials
Parse full JSON response body for passwordHash, password, or hash fields
cases.expected: Response contains token and profile only; no passwordHash, password, or credential material in API response per domain model rule that passwordHash is never exposed

cases.title: Registration validation errors use standard typed error envelope
preconditions: No existing user with target email
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with invalid email format
Parse JSON error body
cases.expected: HTTP 400 with error.code INVALID_INPUT, user-safe error.message, and requestId; no internal stack trace or DB details leaked per error handling contract and NFR-13

cases.title: Duplicate signup with same email is rejected
preconditions: Existing participant account registered with email user@example.com
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with the same email, different password and displayName
cases.expected: HTTP 409 with error.code EMAIL_ALREADY_REGISTERED; no second user row created; original account credentials remain valid

cases.title: Signup with missing or empty displayName is rejected
preconditions: Fresh email not yet registered
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with valid email and password but empty or whitespace-only displayName
cases.expected: HTTP 400 with INVALID_INPUT and displayName field detail; no user row persisted

cases.title: Email normalization treats mixed-case duplicate as same account
preconditions: Existing account registered as user@example.com
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with email User@Example.com and valid password/displayName
cases.expected: HTTP 409 EMAIL_ALREADY_REGISTERED; email uniqueness enforced on normalized lowercase value per domain model and database unique index on lower(email)

cases.title: Concurrent duplicate signup requests for same email yield at most one account
preconditions: No existing user with target email
Ability to fire parallel POST /auth/register requests with identical payload
steps: Send two or more concurrent POST /api/v1/auth/register requests with the same email, password, and displayName
Count successful 200 responses and user rows for that email
cases.expected: Exactly one account created; remaining requests return 409 EMAIL_ALREADY_REGISTERED or equivalent conflict; database unique constraint on email prevents duplicate identity rows

cases.title: Signup form surfaces validation errors for invalid input without navigation
preconditions: Web app running at /signup
Fresh browser context with no stored JWT
steps: Navigate to /signup
Submit form with invalid email format or password below minimum length
Observe inline or form-level error messaging
cases.expected: User remains on signup page; field-level validation errors displayed; no account created and no redirect to protected routes until valid submission

cases.title: Signup with password below minimum length is rejected
preconditions: Fresh email not yet registered
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with valid email and displayName but password shorter than minimum required length
Parse JSON error body
cases.expected: HTTP 400 with error.code INVALID_INPUT and details.field password; no user row persisted; registration rejected at API boundary before persistence
